detaileddescription.para: Save the state to a retained memory space.
Restore the state from a retained memory space.

briefdescription.para: CACHE tasks.
Cache region related to Flash access.
Cache region related to XIP access.
Cache regions.
All RAM is used for cache memory.
Half of the RAM is used for cache memory.
Quarter of the RAM is used for cache memory.
None of the RAM is used for cache memory.
Dedicated RAM size used for cache memory.
Function for enabling the CACHE peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for disabling the CACHE peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the CACHE peripheral is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
The CACHE is enabled.
The CACHE is not enabled.

briefdescription.para: Function for invalidating the cache content.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for erasing the cache content.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking the status of nrf_cache_erase().

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Erase is finished.
Erase is not complete or has not started.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing the status of the cache erase.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the cache profiling.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
True if cache profiling is to be enabled, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing the cache profiling counters.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the number of cache hits for instruction fetch from the specified cache region.

simplesect.para: Separate counters are used for flash region and XIP region.
Cache profiling must be enabled first. See nrf_cache_profiling_set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Cache region.

simplesect.para: Number of instruction fetch cache hits.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the number of cache misses for instruction fetch from the specified cache region.

simplesect.para: Separate counters are used for flash region and XIP region.
Cache profiling must be enabled first. See nrf_cache_profiling_set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Cache region.

simplesect.para: Number of instruction fetch cache misses.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the number of cache hits for data fetch from the specified cache region.

simplesect.para: Separate counters are used for flash region and XIP region.
Cache profiling must be enabled first. See nrf_cache_profiling_set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Cache region.

simplesect.para: Number of data fetch cache hits.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the number of cache misses for data fetch from the specified cache region.

simplesect.para: Separate counters are used for flash region and XIP region.
Cache profiling must be enabled first. See nrf_cache_profiling_set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Cache region.

simplesect.para: Number of data fetch cache misses.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the cache RAM mode.

detaileddescription.para: When configured in the RAM mode, the accesses to internal or external flash will not be cached. In this mode, the cache data contents can be used as the read/write RAM. Only the data content of the cache is available as RAM.

simplesect.para: Enabling the RAM mode causes the RAM to be cleared.
Disabling the RAM mode causes the cache to be invalidated.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
True if the cache RAM mode is to be enabled, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking whether the cache is in RAM mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

simplesect.para: True if the cache RAM mode is enabled, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Function for setting the configuration of splitting the dedicated cache RAM.

detaileddescription.para: Dedicated cache RAM can be splitted into cache memory and generic memory. By default, all dedicated RAM is used for cache memory.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Dedicated cache RAM split configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the configuration of splitting the dedicated cache RAM.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

simplesect.para: Configuration of dedicated cache RAM split.

briefdescription.para: Function for blocking the cache content access.

detaileddescription.para: To unlock the cache content access, a reset has to be performed.

simplesect.para: Blocking is ignored in the RAM mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

briefdescription.para: Function for blocking the cache content updates.

detaileddescription.para: Blocking of updates prevents updating of cache content on cache misses, but the peripheral will continue to check for instruction/data fetches in the content already present in the cache.

simplesect.para: Blocking is ignored in the RAM mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
True if cache content update lock is to be enabled, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (NRF_CACHE_Type const *p_reg, uint32_t set, uint8_t way, uint8_t word)
memberdef.name: nrf_cache_data_get

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the cache data word.

simplesect.para: When operating in the RAM mode, the cache data is accessible as a general purpose RAM.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Set that contains the data to get.
Way that contains the data to get.
Data word index to get.

simplesect.para: 32-bit data word.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the tag associated with the specified set and way.

detaileddescription.para: The tag is used to check if an entry in the cache matches the address that is being fetched.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Set that contains the tag to get.
Way that contains the tag to get.

simplesect.para: Tag value.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking the validity of a cache line associated with the specified set and way.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Set that contains the cache line to check.
Way that contains the cache line to check.
Cache line is valid.
Cache line is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the most recently used way in the specified set.

detaileddescription.para: The most recently used way is updated on each fetch from the cache and is used for the cache replacement policy.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Specified set.

simplesect.para: The most recently used way in the specified set.

memberdef.argsstring: (NRF_CACHE_Type const *p_reg, uint32_t set, uint8_t way, uint8_t word)
memberdef.name: nrf_cache_data_unit_validity_check

briefdescription.para: Function for checking the validity of a data unit associated with the specified set, way and word.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Set that contains the data unit to check.
Way that contains the data unit to check.
Data word index.
Data unit is valid.
Data unit is invalid.

memberdef.argsstring: (NRF_CACHE_Type const *p_reg, uint32_t set, uint8_t way, uint8_t word)
memberdef.name: nrf_cache_is_data_unit_dirty_check

briefdescription.para: Function for checking the dirtiness of a data unit associated with the specified set, way and word.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Set that contains the data unit to check.
Way that contains the data unit to check.
Data word index.
Data unit is dirty.
Data unit is clean.

briefdescription.para: Function to set the memory address covered by the line to be maintained.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Cache line adress.

briefdescription.para: Function to get the memory address covered by the line to be maintained.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

simplesect.para: Cache line adress.

briefdescription.para: Function for triggering the specified CACHE task.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Task.

briefdescription.para: Function for returning the address of the specified task register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.
Task.

simplesect.para: Task address.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if the cache is busy or not.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the structure of registers of the peripheral.

simplesect.para: True if the cache is busy, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Symbol indicating whether CACHEDATA structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether DCACHEDATA structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether ICACHEDATA structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether any CACHEDATA structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether CACHEDATA data units field is present.
Symbol indicating whether CACHEINFO structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether DCACHEINFO structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether ICACHEINFO structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether any CACHEINFO structure is present.
Symbol indicating whether dirtiness check functionality for cache is supported.
Symbol indicating whether data unit validation functionality for cache is supported.
Symbol indicating whether cache info has INFO register.
Symbol indicating whether cache tasks are supported.
Symbol indicating whether the CLEAN cache/line tasks are supported.
Symbol indicating whether the cache ERASE task is supported.
Symbol indicating whether the cache ERASE is supported.
Symbol indicating whether the cache INVALIDATELINE task is supported.
Symbol indicating whether the cache LINEADDR task is supported.
Symbol indicating whether the cache WRITELOCK task is supported.
Symbol indicating whether the FLUSH cache/line tasks are supported.
Symbol indicating whether save and restore tasks are supported.
Symbol indicating whether status check is supported.
Symbol indicating whether splitting the dedicated RAM between cache and generic memory is supported.
Symbol indicating whether configuration of split of dedicated RAM between cache and generic memory is supported.
Symbol indicating whether cache DEBUGLOCK is supported.
Max number of words in CACHEDATA data units.
Max number of CACHEDATA data units.
Max number of CACHEDATA words.
Max number of CACHEDATA ways.
Max number of CACHEDATA sets.
Max number of words in DCACHEDATA data units.
Max number of DCACHEDATA data units.
Max number of DCACHEDATA words.
Max number of DCACHEDATA ways.
Max number of DCACHEDATA sets.
Max number of words in ICACHEDATA data units.
Max number of ICACHEDATA data units.
Max number of ICACHEDATA words.
Max number of ICACHEDATA ways.
Max number of ICACHEDATA sets.
Max number of words in CACHEINFO data units.
Max number of CACHEINFO data units.
Max number of CACHEINFO words.
Max number of CACHEINFO ways.
Max number of CACHEINFO sets.
Max number of words in DCACHEINFO data units.
Max number of DCACHEINFO data units.
Max number of DCACHEINFO words.
Max number of DCACHEINFO ways.
Max number of CACHEINFO sets.
Max number of words in ICACHEINFO data units.
Max number of ICACHEINFO data units.
Max number of ICACHEINFO words.
Max number of ICACHEINFO ways.
Max number of ICACHEINFO sets.
Mask of ICACHEINFO TAG field.
Mask of ICACHEINFO V field.
Position of ICACHEINFO V field.
Valid value of ICACHEINFO V field.
Mask of ICACHEINFO MRU field.
Position of ICACHEINFO MRU field.
Mask of ICACHEINFO DUV0 field.
Position of ICACHEINFO DUV0 field.
Valid value of ICACHEINFO DUV0 field.
Mask of ICACHEINFO DUV1 field.
Position of ICACHEINFO DUV1 field.
Valid value of ICACHEINFO DUV1 field.
Mask of ICACHEINFO DUV2 field.
Position of ICACHEINFO DUV2 field.
Valid value of ICACHEINFO DUV2 field.
Mask of ICACHEINFO DUV3 field.
Position of ICACHEINFO DUV3 field.
Valid value of ICACHEINFO DUV3 field.
Max enumerator value of RAMSIZE field.
The hardware access layer for managing the CACHE peripheral.
